steps.title: Identify fixed and variable costs
steps.content:
The activation cost is a one‑time fee, so it does not depend on the number of months: it is a constant term \$35.
The monthly cost repeats each month, so it is a rate multiplied by the number of months: $23 per month gives $23t.

steps.title: Write the total cost equation
steps.content:
Add the constant and the variable parts:
$$c = 35 + 23t$$
This can also be written as
$$c = 23t + 35$$.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
